topic: Your AI resume is invisible: recruiter teardown
thesis: A funny recruiter-style teardown showing why a polished AI resume can miss HubSpot, CAC, LinkedIn Ads, lifecycle marketing, and pipeline proof.

script.title: Your AI Resume Is Invisible: Recruiter Teardown

chapters.content: Show the free score and paid offer only after the gap is visible.

youtube.title: Your AI Resume Is Invisible: Recruiter Teardown
youtube.seoTitle: Your AI Resume Is Invisible: Recruiter Teardown | Signal by ATSHacker
youtube.description: A recruiter-style resume teardown for frustrated job hunters. We show why a polished AI resume can become invisible, translate a real job description into searchable proof, rewrite one bullet without fake experience, and reveal the Signal score jump from 34/100 to 92/100.
youtube.cta: Paste the job description and check your free Signal score before you apply.

scriptSections.label: Cold open
scriptSections.script: This resume looks clean, polite, and professional, which is exactly the trap. The open loop today is simple: this candidate is probably qualified, but the resume is invisible because the proof is wearing camouflage.
scriptSections.visual: Start on a clean resume, hard zoom to a 34/100 badge, Signal mascot points at the missing proof, then split screen with the job description.

scriptSections.label: Stakes
scriptSections.script: The painful mistake is thinking pretty language equals searchable language. A recruiter does not search for professional fog. They search for a title, tools, outcomes, and proof that matches the job description.
scriptSections.visual: Show a recruiter search box typing Demand Generation Manager, HubSpot, CAC, LinkedIn Ads, lifecycle marketing, and pipeline.

scriptSections.label: Search test
scriptSections.script: For this role, the job description is screaming HubSpot, CAC analysis, LinkedIn Ads, lifecycle marketing, demand generation, pipeline, and measurable campaign performance. Those are the signals we need to find on the resume.
scriptSections.visual: Highlight keyword chips from the job description and move them into a recruiter search board.

scriptSections.label: Resume crime scene
scriptSections.script: Now look at the resume. It says results-driven team player, helped with marketing campaigns, and worked with cross-functional teams. That is not bad experience. That is invisible experience.
scriptSections.visual: Circle each vague bullet with red/yellow annotations and label the page qualified but hard to find.

scriptSections.label: Why it gets missed
scriptSections.script: Hiring teams are not mind readers. If the real work involved HubSpot, LinkedIn Ads, CAC, lifecycle marketing, or pipeline, but the resume hides all of it behind helped with campaigns, the evidence is wearing camouflage.
scriptSections.visual: Animate the good proof fading under vague words, then Signal pulls the terms back into view.

scriptSections.label: Fix the bullet
scriptSections.script: The fix is not inventing experience. The fix is translation. Instead of helped with marketing campaigns, write: cut CAC by 32% through LinkedIn Ads audience segmentation and HubSpot lead scoring.
scriptSections.visual: Type the before bullet, delete it, and replace it with the proof bullet while Signal validates each term.

scriptSections.label: Score reveal
scriptSections.script: Same person. Same work. Completely different signal. The example moves from 34 out of 100 to 92 out of 100 because the resume finally says the tools, metric, and outcome the role asked for.
scriptSections.visual: Score meter jumps from 34/100 to 92/100, with green checks on HubSpot, CAC, LinkedIn Ads, lifecycle marketing, and pipeline.

scriptSections.label: Trust check
scriptSections.script: Here is the line we do not cross: no fake experience, no keyword stuffing, no ATS mythology, and no outcome promises. The resume should become clearer, not fictional.
scriptSections.visual: Show four trust badges: no fake experience, no keyword stuffing, no guarantees, free score first.

scriptSections.label: Signal workflow
scriptSections.script: That is why Signal starts with the free score. Paste the job description, upload the resume, see the gap, and only pay for the resume or cover letter bundle if the gap is worth fixing before you apply.
scriptSections.visual: Show the Signal workflow: resume plus job description, free score, targeted rewrite, resume plus cover letter CTA.

shorts.title: Your AI resume has LinkedIn breath
shorts.hook: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport lounges.
shorts.script: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport lounges. Very polished. Very beige. But the job asks for HubSpot, CAC, and lifecycle marketing, and your resume says results-driven team player. Signal finds the missing role language before you apply.
storyboard: Open with giant text: 'LinkedIn breath detected.'
Show an over-polished AI resume with a beige 'professional yap' stamp.
Cut to job description terms highlighted in yellow.
Signal mascot side-eyes the missing keyword chips.
Reveal free Signal score and CTA.

props.hook1: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport loung
props.subline: Signal compares resume language to the target job.
props.voiceover_text: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport lounges. Very polished. Very beige. But the job asks for HubSpot, CAC, and lifecycle marketing, and your resume says results-driven team player. Signal finds the missing role language before you apply.

shorts.title: Stop writing helped with campaigns
shorts.hook: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache.
shorts.script: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache. 'Helped with campaigns' tells me almost nothing. If you used HubSpot, ran paid social, or moved pipeline, say that clearly. No fake experience. Just stop hiding the good part.
storyboard: Circle weak bullet and stamp 'suspiciously vague'.

props.hook1: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache.
props.subline: Signal compares resume language to the target job.
props.voiceover_text: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache. 'Helped with campaigns' tells me almost nothing. If you used HubSpot, ran paid social, or moved pipeline, say that clearly. No fake experience. Just stop hiding the good part.

shorts.title: The ATS is not a wizard
shorts.hook: The ATS did not read your mind. Rude, honestly.
shorts.script: The ATS did not read your mind. Rude, honestly. But it usually stores and searches what you actually wrote. If the job says lifecycle marketing and your resume says helped the team, your best experience is hiding in a hoodie.

monetization.primaryGoal: Earn trust with a concrete teardown, then route qualified viewers to the free Signal score.
monetization.cta: Paste the job description and check your free Signal score before you apply.
monetization.upsell: After score completion, position the $14.99 resume + cover letter bundle as the best-value fix for a target job.

viewerCustomerReview: Viewer/customer review target: funny enough for frustrated job hunters to keep watching, useful enough to earn trust, and commercial only after the free score gap is obvious.
